key_insight: Orthogonalized enriched finite elements combine the completeness of classical finite elements with compact atom-centered enrichment functions to make pseudopotential DFT faster and more scalable.
engineering_relevance: This framework reduces degrees of freedom, runtime, and memory for large-scale Kohn-Sham DFT, improving feasibility for high-electron-count simulations on parallel computers.
method: Augment classical finite elements with compact atom-centered enrichment functions.
Orthogonalize enrichment functions against the finite element basis to improve conditioning and simplify overlap-matrix inversion.
Use residual-based Chebyshev subspace iteration to solve the Kohn-Sham eigenproblem efficiently despite approximate overlap inversion.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks up to 39,083 electrons show the enriched basis cuts degrees of freedom by 5-7×, computational cost by 5-9×, and memory by 4-5× relative to classical finite elements, while maintaining accuracy and parallel scalability.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate and stable construction of enrichment functions and approximate overlap inversion, so implementation quality may strongly affect conditioning and convergence.
paper_title: Large-scale pseudopotential density functional theory calculations using orthogonalized enriched finite element basis
